title: Triclops
content:

{{Quote|He's like two people living inside the same body—part of him good and well-intentioned; the other part an evil and dangerous genius inventor.|Ken|<ref name="QOE">''[[Queen of the Empire]]''</ref>}}
'''Triclops''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[mutant]] [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] who claimed to be the son of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]].<ref name="SPINDC" /> It is possible that this relation may not have been in a strict biological sense, and that Triclops was the implementation of [[Darth Plagueis/Legends|Darth Plagueis's]] experiments in the influence of [[Midi-chlorian/Legends|midi-chlorians]], with the goal of creating a zygote solely through the use of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] in a fertile [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Gender/Legends|female]].

Triclops was a white-haired mutant with three [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], one being on the back of his head, and sported scars on his temples from [[Shock-therapy|electroshocks]] administered by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. He was trained to be an [[Emperor's Eye]] but Palpatine considered him one of his greatest failures, and had him imprisoned. He languished for years in an Imperial [[Prison/Legends|prison]] on [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]], fathering a son, [[Ken]], with a former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] named [[Kendalina]].

He eventually escaped from the clutches of the Empire and, betraying his father, joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] with his son Ken before disappearing from [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] entirely.

==Biography==
===Early life===
{{Quote|Triclops is the deepest, darkest secret of the Empire. The only Imperials who know of his existence are the most powerful members of the Imperial ruling class, such as the Grand Moffs.|Ken|<ref name="MMY">''[[Mission from Mount Yoda]]''</ref>}}
[[File:SlyMooreStare-OP.png|thumb|left|200px|Sly Moore was rumored to have borne Triclops.]]
According to rumors, the three-[[Eye/Legends|eyed]] mutant known as Triclops was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] to [[Sly Moore/Legends|Sly Moore]], an [[Umbaran/Legends|Umbaran]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] who had been a close adviser of the [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] and then [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. Moore, who had taken great interest in the [[Geneticist/Legends|genetic experiments]] of the late [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth Plagueis/Legends|Darth Plagueis]], had come into the possession of Force-sensitive DNA from an undisclosed source. If rumors could be relied on, she [[Death/Legends|died]] in her native [[Ghost Nebula/Legends|Ghost Nebula]] while [[Reproduction/Legends|giving birth]] to Triclops, and the child was immediately spirited away by her former confederate, [[Sarcev Quest]].<ref name="BT3" /> Since Moore was still alive in [[18 BBY/Legends|18 BBY]], one [[Standard year/Legends|year]] after the [[Declaration of a New Order|establishment]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Triclops would have to be born after that point.<ref name="MoD">''[[The Last of the Jedi: Master of Deception]]''</ref> Regardless of the veracity of that report, Triclops was regarded as the true son of Palpatine,<ref name="SPINDC" /> both by himself and the few high-ranking officials who knew of him.<ref name="PODS">[[Prophets of the Dark Side (novel)|''Prophets of the Dark Side'']]</ref>

Triclops' education was entrusted to the [[Prophets of the Dark Side]], an ancient cult who had pledged allegiance to Palpatine's [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. He was trained to become an [[Emperor's Eye]], a [[Dark side adept/Legends|Dark Side Adept]] with a particular propensity for seeing into [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. However, the three-eyed prophet in training proved to be a disappointment, and the Emperor himself regarded him as a personal failure.<ref name="SPINDC" /><ref name="END" /> According to the [[Bimm/Legends|Bimm]] con artist [[Rajah Ubooki]], who posed as the false [[Supreme Prophet]] [[Kadann]], Palpatine had sensed that Triclops could grow more powerful than he was in the Force, and was also a pacifist who believed in disarmament instead of war.<ref name="SPINDC" /><ref name="PODS" /> Rather than killing him, the Emperor made his own son a common [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] in the [[Spice/Legends|spice]] [[Mining/Legends|mines]] of [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]].<ref name="LCJ">''[[The Lost City of the Jedi]]''</ref>

===Imprisonment===
[[File:Triclops.jpg|thumb|200px|The imprisoned Triclops]]
{{Quote|The Grand Moffs refuse to admit officially that Triclops exists. They believe he's insane, and they're terrified that if he's ever set free, he might take over as ruler of the Empire, and destroy everything in the galaxy, including them!|Ken|<ref name="MMY" />}}
While imprisoned in the Kessel mines asylum, Triclops was often whipped by the [[Supreme Slavelord]] himself, another three-eyed man named [[Trioculus]].<ref name="MMY" /> While the Supreme Slaver was also a byproduct of Palpatine's machinations, albeit via experimentation,<ref name="BT1">{{SW|url=news/barely-tolerable-alien-henchmen-of-the-empire-part-1|series=Barely Tolerable: Alien Henchmen of the Empire|text=Part 1|nolive=1}}</ref> he was jealous of Triclops' position as the Emperor's true son. While whipping him, Trioculus used to swear that he would one day assume Triclops' identity and take over as ruler of the Empire.<ref name="MMY" /> In [[7 BBY/Legends|7 BBY]], Triclops fell in love with [[Kendalina]], a gray-eyed "[[Jedi Princess]]" who had been forced to pledge allegiance to the Empire and assigned to work as a nurse in the asylum. The two produced a child, a boy whom they named [[Ken]]. When the Empire discovered their relationship, they killed Kendalina, an event that was burned into Triclops' memory forever. In secret, the infant Ken was taken to the [[Lost City of the Jedi]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], where he would remain for twelve [[Standard year/Legends|years]], while Triclops remained in the Empire's firm grip. In addition to being regularly [[Torture/Legends|tortured]], Palpatine' son was unwittingly outfitted with a [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]] transceiver [[HoloNet transceiver implant|implant]] in his right upper [[Tooth/Legends|molar]], possibly by [[Cronal|Blackhole]]. That device could not only broadcast Triclops's thoughts to the Imperial probe droids, but also duplicate the mind-controlling effects of [[Nihil smokestone]], sending electromagnetic signals into his [[Brain/Legends|brain]] while he was soundly [[Sleep/Legends|asleep]].

When Palpatine died, rumors abounded that the Emperor had a three-eyed son. However, the Imperial Grand Moffs considered Triclops both insane and dangerous, due in part to his pacifistic views and calls for disarmament. [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Bertroff Hissa]] took advantage of this, setting up Supreme Slavelord Trioculus as Palpatine's heir.

Triclops was later moved to the new [[Imperial Reprogramming Institute]], a [[Prison/Legends|prison]] and lunatic asylum nestled in the [[Valley of Royalty]] of [[Duro/Legends|Duro]], not far from ancient monuments. In the Institute, the Empire kept its most dangerous prisoners. The son of Palpatine was put in [[Experimental Section Two]], where—according to the false Kadann—even the most insane prisoners ultimately learned obedience to the Empire. Triclops was put under the rule of [[Defeen]], a male [[Defel/Legends|Defel]] who was tasked with reprogramming him.<ref name="MMY" />

One day in [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], Triclops finally escaped Experimental Section Two, with the help of an Imperial assassin droid whose assassination protocols had been deactivated. However, Triclops could not pass over [[Great Wall|the Great Wall]] without a spaceship or a floating vehicle, which prompted him to hide somewhere in the Valley of Royalty. With the assistance of the reprogrammed assassin droid, he climbed down the mouth of [[Monument to Queen Rana|the monument]] of [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Rana Mas Trehalt]] in hopes of finding his way to freedom. However, he and the droid got trapped in a series of tunnels instead. He remained there until an intervention team sent by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] discovered the tunnel and freed him. The team in question was made of [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], his wartime friend [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], the [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and a young Human who was none other than Ken, Triclops' long-lost son. Now aligned with his rescuers, Triclops joined the Alliance with the aid of his son and Skywalker, but was held under close supervision by [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] for fear that he was an Imperial spy.<ref name="MMY" />

===Discovery===
{{Quote|I know what a shock it must have been for you to realize that your grandfather was Emperor Palpatine. And the things I must do in the days ahead will surely shock you just as much.|Triclops|<ref name="PODS" />}}
It was around this time that the Empire's reasons for keeping Triclops alive became clear: while asleep, he would [[Dream/Legends|dream]] designs for deadly new machines of war, which the Empire would later produce. During these dreams he would often enter convulsive fits, and even the mighty [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] could be hard-pressed to restrain him. He would also telepathically read the minds of nearby engineers and verbally offered corrections and improvements to their designs. He would not, however, recall the dreams after awakening, never realizing that he was aiding the Empire he despised.

On [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], Triclops, after writing an impassioned letter to Ken, escaped into the jungles of the moon.<ref name="PODS" /> Several months later he became the slave of the false Kadann and helped him escape the [[Lost City of the Jedi]] and make his way offworld.<ref name="who">{{InsiderCite|66|Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals}}</ref> He was last seen when he stormed into the [[Grand Audience Chamber]] of the [[Great Temple/Legends|Massassi Temple]], where the [[Wedding of Han Solo and Leia Organa (5 ABY)|wedding ceremony]] of [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] was about to start, and attacked the attendants in a fit of madness induced by his mind controlling implant.<ref name="SPINDC" />

==Behind the scenes==
===Origins===
[[File:Davelandpalpatines.jpg|thumb|right|[[David Land]] introduces the audience to Triclops and Ken in a non-canon introduction to ''Star Wars Tales 14''.]]
Triclops was created by [[Paul Davids|Paul]] and [[Hollace Davids]] for their young-readers [[Adventures of a Jedi Prince|''Jedi Prince'']] series. He was first mentioned in ''[[The Glove of Darth Vader]]'',<ref name="GODV">''[[The Glove of Darth Vader]]''</ref> but made his sole appearances in the flesh in the three last books of the series, ''[[Mission from Mount Yoda]]'',<ref name="MMY" /> ''[[Queen of the Empire]]'',<ref name="QOE" /> and [[Prophets of the Dark Side (novel)|''Prophets of the Dark Side'']].<ref name="PODS" />

===Controversy over ascendancy===
In the ''Jedi Prince'' series, Triclops was introduced as the son of Emperor Palpatine, which author Paul Davids intended to be the literal [[in-universe]] truth.<ref name="SAVR">{{WebCite|url=http://www.scumandvillainyradio.com/sidebar/jedi-prince-part-2-episode-8-for-1172014|text=Jedi Prince, Part 2 (Episode #8 for 1/17/2014)|work=Scum and Villainy Radio|nobackup=1}}</ref> However, in [[2007]], authors [[Abel G. Peña]] and [[Rich Handley]] wrote ''[[Aliens in the Empire]]'', an online supplement to [[Star Wars Insider 96|''Star Wars Insider'' 96]] and ''[[Star Wars Insider 97|97]]'', which cast doubt on the literalness of the phrase "son of Palpatine." The article mentioned a three-eyed mutant who was born from a [[Bordal]]i woman named [[Niobi]] and was the result of Imperial genetic engineering.<ref name="Hyp">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/96/indexp2.html|series=Aliens in the Empire|text=Part 1: Hierarchy of Power}}</ref> At the time, Peña hinted on the [[Jedi Council Forums]] that the mutant in question was Triclops.<ref name="JCF">{{JCFCite|url=posts/27723870|board=Literature|thread=Aliens of the Empire (Part 2 now up)|user=[[Abel G. Peña|Halagad_Ventor]]|date=2007-11-10|time=4:07 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304014652/http://boards.theforce.net/threads/aliens-of-the-empire-part-2-now-up.27707732/page-3}}</ref>

However, in [[2013]], Peña and Handley wrote a new series of articles for the official [[Star Wars Blog]], entitled ''[[Barely Tolerable: Alien Henchmen of the Empire]]''. Part one of that series revealed that the three-eyed son of Niobi was in fact Trioculus,<ref name="BT1" /> which disproved what had been speculated on the Jedi Council Forums.<ref name="JCF" />

In part three of the article series, which was released on [[September 12]], 2013, Handley and Peña tied Triclops with Sly Moore, but did not explicitly reveal the father's identity.<ref name="BT3" /> Later in the year, Peña and [[Greg Mitchell]] co-wrote another blog article titled ''[[The Star Wars Spy Game: SPIN Declassified]]'', which explicitly referred to Triclops as Palpatine's true son, not simply an alleged one.<ref name="SPINDC" />
